item.title: KKR eyes multibillion-dollar sale of data centre cooling company
item.description: Private capital firm seeks big return on investment in CoolIT Systems as AI boom boosts valuations

item.title: Gulf businesses buy up political violence insurance as conflict spreads
item.description: Data centres, energy projects and hotels move to limit potential losses

item.title: Liberals should be less shy about saying what they stand for
item.description: Failure to defend their ideology has created an intellectual vacuum on the American left

item.title: Car insurance bills are going up before they go down
item.description: Costs have been driven up by a combination of skills shortages, supply-chain issues and complicated modern cars

item.title: Geopolitical hit to travel is ‘temporary’, says CEO of world’s largest visa processor
item.description: VFS boss Zubin Karkaria is confident global migration will remain resilient despite new tensions

item.title: Samsung seeks AI deals to challenge Apple’s smartphone lead
item.description: Korean giant’s device chief says its future Galaxy devices will host multiple models as users mix and match AI tools

item.title: The ‘economic heroes’ driving Indonesia
item.description: A merger between two big ride-hailing companies could make life harder for drivers

item.title: Why it’s time to end the grim march of the touchscreen
item.description: Self-service kiosks make us buy more stuff and feel more lonely

item.title: Ukraine’s drone interceptors draw U.S. and Gulf interest, but wartime ban blocks sales
item.description: Kyiv banned weapons exports when the war began in 2022

item.description: Iran’s president apologises to Gulf neighbours for attacking them

item.title: In the Iran war, good news is bad news for oil, Canada and our future
item.description: The longer the conflict continues, the more it’s looking like an act of economic self-harm by the U.S., and Canadians will be collateral damage

item.title: ‘This time could be different’: How Canada is bulking up business ties with India
item.description: India recently overtook Japan as the world’s fourth largest economy and represents a huge opportunity for Canadian businesses

item.title: Fallout from Iran war complicating Ottawa’s push to tap into Middle East investment pools
item.description: Emerging financial capitals are on edge as the conflict jeopardizes oil and gas supplies coming out of the Gulf

item.title: Higher sales, same headcount: How AI is transforming work at Canada’s biggest companies
item.description: Many of the country’s biggest tech firms have boasted about their efficiency gains from AI

item.title: US faced with few good options to tamp down surging oil prices
item.description: Experts say reopening Strait of Hormuz remains most viable option as ideas such as influencing futures market fade

item.description: The conflict with Iran is unpopular with the American public and is roiling markets and the economy

item.description: Experts say reopening Strait of Hormuz remains most viable option as ideas such as influencing futures market fade

item.title: First Brands creditors reckon with dwindling chance of repayment
item.description: Upcoming asset sales are expected to yield as little as $200mn to pay off debt pile of more than $12bn

item.description: An inside look at two winners’ strategies and what they learned from the Trade Off competition

item.title: Chart of the Week: Europe’s extra energy tax
item.description: A weaker euro deepens the pain from higher oil prices

item.title: Price is now the main ingredient in the office-lunch slop bowl
item.description: Many chains have raised prices to absorb rising costs — which works as long as customer loyalty, or indifference, endures

item.description: From AI weapons to Game Boys, defence tech billionaire is in talks to raise funds for new gaming venture ModRetro

item.title: Meteorologists bet on ‘swing’ to warming El Niño weather cycle
item.description: Chance of natural cycle that raises equatorial ocean temperatures put at 59% between August and October

item.title: UK lenders raise mortgage rates amid warnings over inflation and energy prices
item.description: Market mood ‘has completely changed’ following outbreak of Middle East war

item.title: Can Crispin Odey convince a judge he was the victim of a regulatory crusade?
item.description: Financier is appealing UK watchdog’s decision to ban and fine him in the wake of sexual misconduct allegations

item.description: The move might help asset managers but hurt savers and the economy more broadly

item.description: A protracted conflict in the Middle East would dent expectations that global coal consumption will start to fall

item.title: ‘Being honest about my debts is helping others — and it’s helped me’

item.description: Effective marginal tax rates discourage higher earners from pursuing career advancementTax

item.description: Millions of people are already turning to chatbots such as ChatGPT to plan their retirement

item.description: New roles from Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment offset reductions in other parts of the company

item.description: Goldman Sachs warns crude will soar past 2008 peak if action is not taken to reopen Strait of Hormuz

item.description: Governments say deal will bring ‘one project, one review’ approach to addressing impact of projects

item.description: Chambers of commerce urge Ottawa to ban children under 16 from using AI tools and social media

item.description: Non-profit has already raised $1-million for project helping young adults and families find mental-health services they need

item.title: The Iran war is propelling Germany into another energy crisis
item.description: Situation is made worse by Berlin’s energy policy for the past 25 years, which has been illogical to the point of madness

item.description: Penalty is the largest ever imposed against a broker-dealer for violating U.S. Bank Secrecy Act
